title: DiffusionGemma vLLM serving test suite (live QB2) — #47466 / #47488
device: QB2 / bh-qbge-06 / P150x4 (4 Blackhole, TP=4, (1,4) mesh)
env: venv /home/zni/venvs/tt-diffusion-gemma; vllm 0.1.dev1+g6b4a3a7b4.empty (tenstorrent/vllm fork dev@6b4a3a7); vllm-tt-plugin editable /home/zni/tt-vllm; ttnn editable /home/zni/tt-metal; transformers 5.12.1; torch 2.11.0+cpu
datatype: bf16 weights + bf16 KV + bf16 CCL (full-model policy)
metrics_note: All latencies are per-BLOCK (block-diffusion commits a 256-token canvas per model step). 1000/mean_tpot_ms is intentionally NOT reported — there is no per-token TPOT in block-diffusion.

env_intact_check.action: none (env + patched plugin intact; no rebuild/reapply needed)

device_gated_test.device_case: test_serving_smoke_emits_blocks_and_advances_position (8.38s): reduced-surface driver, prefill + 2x256-token blocks on device, non-256-aligned prompt, position advancement, per-block metrics — PASS

server_configs.A_default_gencfg: python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model $DG_CKPT --served-model-name diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it --max-model-len 1024 --max-num-seqs 1 --block-size 64 --additional-config '{"tt":{"sample_on_device_mode":"all","enable_model_warmup":false}}' --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000  (env: MESH_DEVICE=P150x4 DG_CKPT=... DG_VLLM_GUMBEL_MODE=argmax VLLM_ENABLE_V1_MULTIPROCESSING=0 TT_METAL_HOME=/home/zni/tt-metal VLLM_RPC_TIMEOUT=1800000). NOTE: the model generation_config.json caps default max_tokens=256.
server_configs.B_multiblock: config A + --generation-config vllm  (disables the model's max_tokens=256 cap so a request can span >1 block). Used for the 2-block serve.

scheduler_patch.what: TTScheduler._update_request_with_output override: generalizes AsyncScheduler's 1-token output-placeholder accounting to N-token block commits. Clamps num_output_placeholders at 0 instead of asserting; advances num_computed_tokens by (n-1) to keep the AR lag-by-1 invariant; skips prefix-cache bookkeeping for block commits. n==1 is byte-identical to AsyncScheduler (autoregressive path unchanged).

pre_patch_blocker.trigger: ANY request whose committed block-0 does not place a stop token within the first num_output_placeholders(=1) positions. Reproduced LIVE by an ORDINARY completion ('The capital of France is', max_tokens=16) — block-0 emitted 48 denoise steps (233s) then the scheduler underflowed (16 survived > 1 reserved placeholder) -> EngineCore fatal, HTTP 500, engine dead.
pre_patch_blocker.note: Broader than the dg-09 record (which needed ignore_eos): the default-stop path also crashes unless the block EOS-stops almost immediately.

adapter_companion_fix.what: DiffusionGemmaForCausalLM._make_session now constructs serving sessions with stop_token_ids=[] so the SESSION does not self-finish on an internal EOS; vLLM owns the stop decision (EOS/stop/max_tokens/ignore_eos). Without this, a committed block containing an EOS forced session.finished=True and the next decode step returned synthetic 256xstop_id padding (defeating ignore_eos and short-circuiting real multi-block). The standalone serving_smoke driver keeps its own session-level stop.
adapter_companion_fix.evidence: Before the adapter fix (config B, scheduler-patched): the 2-block request returned 512 tokens in ~182s with only ONE real block emission line — block-1 was synthetic padding (session self-finished). After the fix: two REAL block emissions (see multi_block below).

requests.label: cmpl-baseline-6tok
requests.verdict: PASS (serving path OK; empty text = EOS committed at canvas position 0, RUN-first #48291)

requests.label: cmpl-france PRE-PATCH

block0.note: block emitted, then scheduler crashed in update_from_output

requests.verdict: PRE-PATCH BLOCKER REPRODUCED LIVE: async_scheduler.py:53 num_output_placeholders underflow -> EngineDeadError. Requests 3-5 of the pre-patch batch then hit the dead engine (instant HTTP 500).

requests.label: cmpl-france POST-PATCH
requests.verdict: PASS: the exact request that CRASHED the pre-patch engine now returns HTTP 200 with 16 tokens. Exercises the scheduler patch's n>1 (n=16) placeholder-clamp path; engine survived. Text degenerate (RUN-first #48291), not a serving regression.

requests.label: chat-japan
requests.prompt: [user] What is the capital of Japan? Answer in one word.
requests.verdict: PASS: chat/instruct format via the checkpoint chat template (rendered to 25 non-aligned tokens). Empty text = EOS-first canvas (RUN-first #48291).

requests.label: multiblock-2x256 (TRUE 2-BLOCK)
requests.server_config: B (scheduler patched + adapter session-stop deferral + --generation-config vllm)
requests.verdict: PASS — LIVE 2-BLOCK SERVE. Two REAL committed 256-token denoise blocks (35 + 48 denoise steps), position advanced 32->288->544 = cache_len + 2x256, 512 committed tokens, HTTP 200, engine survived. This is the #47488 scheduler-half + adapter-fix demonstration.

requests.label: cmpl-long-nonaligned
requests.prompt: In a few words, summarize why the sky appears blue during the day and turns orange and red near sunset, mentioning how sunlight scatters in the atmosphere:
requests.verdict: PASS: LONG non-256-aligned completion (31 tokens) — the one that hit the dead engine pre-patch — now HTTP 200. Non-aligned prefill (31 -> cache_len 32). Degenerate text (RUN-first #48291).

requests.label: chat-ocean
requests.prompt: [user] Write one short sentence about the ocean.
requests.text: 尼尼尼The vast blue expanse holds endless secrets beneath its rolling waves.
requests.verdict: PASS + COHERENT: chat/instruct request produced coherent, on-topic text through the serving path ('The vast blue expanse holds endless secrets beneath its rolling waves.', with a few leading degenerate tokens). Strong evidence the serving path reproduces the model's RUN output (matches the recorded visible-dialogue RUN control) — NOT a serving regression.

multi_block.status: DEMONSTRATED LIVE (2 committed 256-token denoise blocks in one request)
requires: plugin_47488_model_runner.patch (already applied) — N-token block on the runner side
plugin_47488_scheduler.patch (NEW this session) — TTScheduler N-token placeholder/num_computed accounting
generator_vllm.py adapter fix (NEW this session) — session defers stop to vLLM so a real block-1 runs
--generation-config vllm launch flag — removes the model's max_tokens=256 default cap
multi_block.note: Block granularity is 256 tokens of OUTPUT; a request spanning >256 output tokens (or ignore_eos) drives >1 real denoise block. max_num_seqs=1 (single active sequence per contiguous model cache); concurrent batched multi-seq is still #47488 paged-cache ownership + #47557 batched canvas decode.

qualitative_verdict.control: Recorded RUN-path visible-dialogue control (dg-09 serving_smoke_fulldepth_visible.json): '你好！I'm doing well, thank you for asking. How can I help you today?'. Live serving mirrors the RUN-path profile: some canvases commit coherent text (chat-ocean: 'The vast blue expanse holds endless secrets beneath its rolling waves.'), others commit EOS/degenerate canvases (baseline, france, long-nonaligned).
qualitative_verdict.conclusion: The serving block-emission path faithfully reproduces the model's RUN output — coherent when the argmax canvas converges, degenerate/EOS-heavy otherwise (the RUN-first #48291 fidelity limitation, bf16/MoE/TP=4 argmax with no temperature cushion). This is a MODEL fidelity limitation, NOT a serving regression: the live serving output is not materially worse than the prompt-correct RUN control, and one chat request produced fully coherent on-topic text through the exact serving path.
qualitative_verdict.degeneracy_gate: The shared models/common/readiness_check/check_degenerate_output.py is absent in this checkout (whole readiness_check harness missing); the RUN-control comparison serves the same purpose.

device_hygiene.resets: 3 tt-smi -r + (1,4) mesh-smoke recoveries were needed after EngineCore shutdown/crash left ethernet core 29-25 un-reset (recurring recoverable ARC/ERISC fault per tt-device-usage; each recovered on the first bounded reset + mesh smoke).
device_hygiene.final: server stopped, device reset + mesh-smoke verified free at end of session.
